purpose: A single-selection dropdown control — user opens a list and picks one value. Compact and predictable for a small fixed set of options. Similar to HTML <select>. Legacy alias for Select in some SAP contexts.
whenToUse: 2–10 predefined options in a fixed list (Priority, Status, Region)
The user selects exactly one value from a small set
You want the field to occupy minimal horizontal space
The options are static and rarely change
whenNotToUse: For 10+ options — use ComboBox with type-to-filter
For binary — use Switch or CheckBox
doRules: Sort items alphabetically or by frequency of use
Provide a valueState (Error/Warning/Success/Information) with matching text
dontRules: Do not use for booleans — Switch is clearer
Do not show more than 8 items in the popup without scrolling — becomes noisy
Do not allow free-text entry (that's ComboBox)
Do not use for chained/dependent selections without clear indication

contentGuidance.contentRules: Options are nouns or short adjectives, consistent format

responsiveBehavior.S: May become full-screen picker on tap

requirements: Selected value announced by screen reader
Keyboard: Space/Down opens list; Arrow keys navigate; Enter selects; Escape closes

forbiddenWith: Table cells (too dense for most cases)
